```json
[
  {
    "idea": "Utility Demand Weather Whipsaw",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "Open-Meteo daily temperature and heating/cooling degree days via weather_series adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Sudden swings in seasonal temperature create rapid demand shocks for electricity and natural gas, forcing utility operators to adjust generation and buying patterns within hours.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLU",
      "rationale": "Utilities rally on unexpected demand spikes that increase margin and utilization rates."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If 7-day rolling average temperature change exceeds ±8°F from prior week and XLU volume spikes 30% above 20-day average",
      "exit": "After 8 trading days or when temperature stabilizes within 5°F of seasonal norm",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Weather volatility occurs weekly in most regions; temperature swings of 8+ degrees happen multiple times per season."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Gold Volatility Vol-of-Vol Spike",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "Yahoo Finance daily prices for GLD and USO via price_only adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When the intraday volatility of gold prices begins spiking (vol-of-vol), it signals macro uncertainty; this often precedes a reallocation into defensive commodities.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLK",
      "rationale": "Tech and growth equities underperform when gold volatility rises, signaling flight-to-safety behavior."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If 5-day rolling standard deviation of GLD daily returns exceeds 2x the 60-day rolling mean, AND GLD closes above its 20-day MA",
      "exit": "After 7 trading days or when vol-of-vol reverts to mean (5-day std dev falls below 1.5x 60-day mean)",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Volatility spikes in commodities occur multiple times per month; gold vol-of-vol thresholds trigger 2-4 times quarterly."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Mortgage Refinance Wave Google Trends Surge",
    "family": "consumer_stress",
    "data": {
      "source": "Google Trends weekly search volume for 'refinance mortgage' via google_trends adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When mortgage refinance searches spike, it signals households are actively managing debt stress; subsequent weeks show either housing demand relief (XLY bullish) or wallet tightening (XLP bullish).",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Successful refinances free up monthly cash flow for discretionary consumer spending."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly Google Trends interest for 'refinance mortgage' spikes 50%+ above 13-week rolling average",
      "exit": "After 3 weeks or when XLY outperforms SPY by 2% cumulatively",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "medium",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Refinance search surges occur 2-3 times per quarter when rate expectations shift."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Airline Load Factor Collapse Bearish Play",
    "family": "travel_mobility",
    "data": {
      "source": "BTS weekly airline load factor via bts_airline_load_factor adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When load factors (seats sold / seats available) drop 3+ percentage points week-over-week, airlines cut capacity and raise fares; near-term bookings weaken, signaling demand deterioration.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Airline load factor weakness signals recession-like travel demand pullback affecting leisure and hospitality."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly BTS load factor falls 3+ points vs. prior week and falls below its 8-week rolling average",
      "exit": "After 4 weeks or when load factor recovers 2+ points from entry level",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "medium",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Load factor swings of 3+ points occur most months; seasonal dips create reliable signal fire."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Small-Cap Growth Momentum Rotation",
    "family": "attention_sentiment",
    "data": {
      "source": "Yahoo Finance daily prices for IWM (Russell 2000) and QQQ via price_only adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When IWM (small-cap) 5-day momentum outpaces QQQ (large-cap growth) by 2% or more, it signals a rotation into lower-liquidity names—often driven by retail attention shifts visible in option flow and social media chatter.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Small-cap rotation favors consumer discretionary and beaten-down cyclical names."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If IWM 5-day return minus QQQ 5-day return exceeds +2% AND IWM volume is 15%+ above 20-day average",
      "exit": "After 6 trading days or if relative momentum reverses (IWM underperforms by 1% or more)",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Small-cap momentum surges occur 2-3 times per month in typical market conditions."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Warehouse Worker Job Posting Surge",
    "family": "labor_jobs",
    "data": {
      "source": "Indeed.com job posting weekly counts for 'warehouse worker' via html_table scrape adapter",
      "type": "scrape",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Sudden spikes in warehouse job postings signal supply chain operators are ramping hiring ahead of expected demand; this precedes retail sales strength by 3-6 weeks.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Warehouse hiring surge indicates businesses expect strong consumer demand in coming weeks."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly warehouse worker postings spike 40%+ above 8-week rolling average",
      "exit": "After 5 weeks or when postings revert to mean",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Warehouse hiring surges occur ahead of peak seasons (holiday, summer) and demand shocks; typically 2-3 spikes per year, each lasting 4-6 weeks."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Freight Rail Carload Collapse Signal",
    "family": "freight_logistics",
    "data": {
      "source": "Association of American Railroads weekly carload data via fred_series adapter (AAR feeds FRED)",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When weekly carloads (cars loaded with freight) drop 8%+ vs. year-ago week, it signals industrial production weakness; this often precedes equity weakness in materials and industrials.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLI",
      "rationale": "Carload collapse is a leading indicator of manufacturing and construction slowdown."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly carloads fall 8%+ year-over-year AND are below their 13-week rolling average",
      "exit": "After 6 weeks or when carloads stabilize above the 13-week average",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "medium",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Carload data is weekly and volatile; seasonal dips and cyclical weakness create multiple entry points per quarter."
  },
  {
    "idea": "EV Charger Installation Acceleration Play",
    "family": "local_economy_weirdness",
    "data": {
      "source": "OpenChargeMap weekly charger count growth rate via openchargemap adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When the weekly rate of new EV charger installations accelerates (week-over-week growth jumps 20%+), it signals municipal/private infrastructure spending surge; this often precedes renewable energy demand and tech equipment procurement.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLK",
      "rationale": "EV charger deployment requires semiconductors, software, and networking gear; acceleration signals tech capex cycle."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly charger installation count growth rate jumps 20%+ above prior week AND cumulative monthly new installations exceed prior 4-week average by 25%",
      "exit": "After 5 weeks or when growth rate decelerates below 10%",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Charger deployment is accelerating nationally; municipal funding tranches and private buildouts create monthly spikes in growth rates."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Financial Sector Volatility Crush Trade",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "Yahoo Finance daily prices for XLF and VIX via price_only adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When VIX drops 3+ points in a single day while the financial sector (XLF) simultaneously gaps down, it signals a washout in financial stocks despite falling macro volatility—often creating a mean-reversion bounce within days.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLF",
      "rationale": "Financial stocks are oversold when VIX falls but sector falls harder; mean reversion favors the sector."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If VIX falls 3+ points day-over-day AND XLF underperforms SPY by 2%+ on the same day AND XLF closes near its lows",
      "exit": "After 5 trading days or once XLF closes 1.5%+ above entry",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "VIX daily moves of 3+ points occur several times per month; financial sector gaps down on these days frequently."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Earthquake Swarm Near Oil Refinery Cluster Signal",
    "family": "local_economy_weirdness",
    "data": {
      "source": "USGS earthquake activity daily data via earthquake_activity adapter, filtered for Texas Gulf Coast and Southern California refinery zones",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When a cluster of 5+ small earthquakes (magnitude 3.0-4.5) occurs within 50 km of a major US refinery zone in a single week, it signals potential refinery operational stress or community concern; downstream energy prices rise and energy stocks underperform.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLE",
      "rationale": "Seismic activity near refineries creates operational risk perception and potential supply disruption fears."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If 5+ earthquakes magnitude 3.0-4.5 occur within 50 km of major refinery cluster in a single week, AND XLE volume exceeds 20-day average by 25%",
      "exit": "After 4 weeks or when earthquake activity returns to background rates (1-2 events per week in zone)",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "medium",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Earthquake swarms near refinery zones occur 1-2 times per quarter; seismic activity is persistent and measurable."
  }
]
```
